what: zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
title: zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
text: Actigraphy data parsing analysis R.  [!WARNING] zeitR early development formally tested. API may change without notice, computed variables yet validated reference implementations, package undergone peer review. Use caution verify outputs independently using research context.

title: zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
text: zeitR R package importing, parsing, analysing raw actigraphy recordings. reads common device output formats, computes standard non-parametric rest-activity variables (, IV, RA, L5, M10), assembles tidy data frames ready downstream chronobiological analysis. zeitR designed complement slumbR Circadia Lab ecosystem: slumbR handles sleep diary questionnaire data, zeitR handles actigraphy side study, packages speak tidy, pipeline-friendly R idioms.

title: zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
text: 📥 read_actigraphy() — parse single actigraphy file structured zeitr_recording object 📂 read_actigraphy_dir() — batch-read whole directory files zeitr_study 📐 compute_npcra() — non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis: , IV, RA, L5, M10 🌅 compute_rest_activity() — derive rest activity onset/offset, total rest time, fragmentation indices 🔀 epoch_resample() — resample recordings target epoch length 📊 study_summary() — participant-level summary recording quality key variables

title: zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
code: zeitR/ ├── R/ │   ├── zeitR-package.R       # package-level docs and imports │   ├── import.R              # read_actigraphy(), read_actigraphy_dir() │   ├── compute.R             # compute_npcra(), compute_rest_activity() │   ├── resample.R            # epoch_resample() │   ├── tidy.R                # study_summary(), print/summary S3 methods │   └── utils.R               # internal helpers ├── man/figures/ │   └── logo.svg              # hex sticker ├── man/                      # roxygen2-generated documentation ├── vignettes/ │   └── getting-started.Rmd  # end-to-end worked example ├── tests/testthat/ │   └── test-package.R ├── .github/workflows/ │   ├── R-CMD-check.yaml │   └── pkgdown.yaml ├── DESCRIPTION ├── LICENSE ├── NEWS.md └── zeitR.Rproj

title: zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
text: R ≥ 4.1 following packages (installed automatically): dplyr, tidyr, lubridate, purrr, rlang, cli

title: zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
code: library(zeitR)  # ── Single recording ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── rec <- read_actigraphy("recordings/P001.csv")  rec$epochs      # tidy epoch-level data frame (timestamp, activity, ...) rec$metadata    # device info, epoch length, recording window  # ── Non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis ──────────────────────────────── npcra <- compute_npcra(rec) npcra #>   participant_id    IS    IV    RA    L5  M10 #>            P001  0.72  0.43  0.89  12.3  84.7  # ── Whole study ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── study <- read_actigraphy_dir("recordings/") study_summary(study) #>   participant_id n_days mean_IS mean_IV mean_RA ...

title: zeitR — Actigraphy parsing and analysis for R
text: compute_npcra() returns standard non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis variables: variables follow definitions Van Someren et al. (1999) Marler et al. (2006).

what: zeitR: Actigraphy Data Parsing and Analysis for R — zeitR-package
title: zeitR: Actigraphy Data Parsing and Analysis for R — zeitR-package
text: Provides tools importing, parsing, analysing raw actigraphy data. Supports common device file formats, computes standard rest-activity variables (, IV, RA, L5, M10), assembles tidy data frames ready downstream chronobiological analysis. Designed complement slumbR Circadia Lab ecosystem.

title: zeitR: Actigraphy Data Parsing and Analysis for R — zeitR-package
text: Maintainer: Lucas França lucas.franca@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID) Authors: Mario Leocadio-Miguel mario.miguel@northumbria.ac.uk (ORCID)

title: zeitR 0.1.0 (2026-06)
text: Initial package scaffold. Core import, rest-activity variable computation, non-parametric circadian rhythm analysis functions follow.
